summary.intent: Implement task #216: a new framework-agnostic ToggleGroup core component under crates/ars-components/src/utility/toggle_group/mod.rs, following the existing spec and TDD workflow, including tests, snapshots, proptest invariants, post-implementation audit, and a committed PR.
summary.outcome: ToggleGroup core fully implemented and committed. All verification gates passed: 50 unit tests, anatomy spec-conformance test, 256-case proptest invariant, snapshot reject gate (2583 tests), spec validation, and mutation testing (196 mutants, 0 missed). PR pushed with Closes #216.

repo: Key is sourced from ars_collections, not ars_core — spec examples importing ars_core::Key were incorrect and required fixing in toggle-group.md, leptos, and dioxus adapter specs.
cargo xci-fast is the required 10-step pre-push gate (fmt, check, clippy, unit, i18n unit, etc.); it must pass before committing after user review approval.
cargo +nightly fmt --all can introduce unrelated formatting churn in other crates (e.g., crates/ars-dioxus/tests/attr_passthrough.rs) during xci-fast; these must be manually reverted before staging.
Snapshot files (.snap) require the snapshot-reviewed label on the PR per repo policy.
Proptest regression seeds are stored in crates/ars-components/proptest-regressions/state-machines.txt and must be committed alongside new proptest tests.
Both Leptos and Dioxus adapter specs must be updated for controlled/uncontrolled prop behavior drift when a new agnostic core is added.
GitHub project item status must be moved to In Progress (via gh project item-edit) before editing code, using the project field and option IDs.
cargo xtask spec validate and cargo xtask spec toc are the spec consistency gates; spec changes must pass both before the implementation is considered complete.
The post-implementation audit skill requires at least two rounds of spec/API drift checking plus llvm-cov coverage and cargo mutants mutation testing with 0 missed.

code.finding: Roving tabindex fallback bug: a controlled value can reference an unregistered item, leaving all rendered items at tabindex=-1. Fix anchors to first enabled registered item when no registered selected item exists.

Blur must repair stale idle context: if Blur fires while state is already Idle but focused_item/focus_visible are non-default, those fields must be cleared. Mutation testing (line 592:49) caught this missing invariant.

Controlled-mode normalization: when selection_mode changes via SetProps while the group is controlled, the controlled value must be normalized to the new mode immediately in sync_props_plan, not deferred to the next SetValue.

workflow: cargo mutants without --jobs N is very slow on large files; use --jobs 8 for parallel evaluation to reduce wall time significantly.
First full mutation pass surfaces the complete survivor set; fixing all survivors at once before a second pass is more efficient than iterative single-mutant fixes.
INSTA_UPDATE=always cargo test <specific_test> is the correct way to accept new insta snapshots for a targeted test rather than running the full suite.
Mutation testing should be run after all other gates pass; survivors indicate genuine test gaps (not equivalent mutants) when the implementation is sound.
Per-field prop sync tests are needed for OR-chain conditions; a single test that changes all fields at once will leave individual field survivors in mutation testing.

Keydown dispatch must be tested as a full matrix (horizontal LTR, horizontal RTL, vertical, Home, End, Space, Enter, ignored cross-axis) rather than a single representative case to kill all keydown handler mutants.

friction: cargo-mutants sequential run (no --jobs) took extremely long for 196 mutants — required extensive write_stdin polling across dozens of turns before the terminal summary appeared.
Two overlapping cargo test commands were accidentally launched simultaneously, causing one to wait on the other's build lock.
cargo xci-fast formatter introduced unrelated churn in crates/ars-dioxus/tests/attr_passthrough.rs that had to be detected manually and reverted before staging.
Mutation testing required three passes (40 missed → 1 missed → 0 missed) due to discovering survivors incrementally rather than all at once, adding significant wall time.
write_stdin polling was the only mechanism to observe long-running cargo-mutants output, requiring dozens of polling calls with no timeout feedback.
open_items: Leptos and Dioxus adapter components for ToggleGroup are explicitly out of scope for this task and not yet implemented.
The PR needs the snapshot-reviewed label applied since .snap files changed — this was noted but depended on the post-push flow.
Codex review loop (waiting-for-codex-review skill, inline thread replies, re-trigger until 👍) must be run after the PR is opened.
llvm-cov coverage report still emits an existing <WORKSPACE>/src/macros.rs source warning unrelated to ToggleGroup — pre-existing issue, not introduced by this task.
Adapter spec drift notes were added to Leptos and Dioxus adapter specs but the adapter component implementations remain unscheduled.
